author: Maya R.
text: I was skeptical at first, but after 8 minutes on the Horizon my Ethiopian natural opened up with jasmine and apricot I'd never tasted before. Completely changed how I cup this bean.

author: Hiroshi T.
text: The reduction in bitterness on my dark-roast espresso shots is remarkable. Crema is fuller, and the aftertaste is cleaner. My regulars noticed before I told them anything.

author: Elena V.
text: Used Horizon on a 3-year-old bag of Panama geisha I had given up on. Five minutes later it tasted alive again. Saved me from tossing a $70 bag.

author: Daniel K.
text: Running side-by-side cuppings at our roastery. The clarity on washed Kenyans is the most consistent shift we've seen. Acidity stays bright but rounder.

author: Priya S.
text: Small detail, but the build quality feels right. Sits on my bar without looking out of place next to the EK43. Worth mentioning alongside the taste results.

author: Tomas L.
text: Honest take: on light-roast pour-overs the difference is subtle. But on moka pot brews at home? Night and day. I'd buy it just for that use case.
